description: Backend server starts and health check endpoint responds with 200 OK
steps: Step 1: Start the API server with `pnpm turbo dev --filter=colloquium-chat-api`
Step 2: Send GET request to http://localhost:5001/api/health
Step 3: Verify response status is 200
Step 4: Verify response body contains { status: 'ok' }

description: Register form (React Hook Form + Zod) submits valid fields and redirects on success
steps: Step 1: Start both frontend and API servers
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:5173/register
Step 3: Fill email field with 'testuser@example.com'
Step 4: Fill username field with 'testuser'
Step 5: Fill password field with 'SecurePass123!'
Step 6: Fill confirm-password field with 'SecurePass123!'
Step 7: Click the 'Create account' button
Step 8: Verify redirect to /w/new or the workspace creation page
Step 9: Verify no React Hook Form field-level error messages are shown beneath any input
Step 10: Verify the user exists in the database with a hashed (not plain) password

description: Register form displays server error for duplicate email via React Hook Form setError
steps: Step 1: Register a user with email 'dup@example.com'
Step 2: Attempt to register another user with the same email 'dup@example.com'
Step 3: Verify the API returns 409 status code
Step 4: Verify React Hook Form renders the server-returned 409 error message beneath the email field

description: User can log in with valid credentials and receives JWT cookies
steps: Step 1: Register a user with email 'login@example.com' and password 'Pass123!'
Step 2: Navigate to /login
Step 3: Fill email with 'login@example.com' and password with 'Pass123!'
Step 4: Click 'Sign in'
Step 5: Verify redirect away from /login
Step 6: Verify the browser has a Set-Cookie header with 'access_token' (httpOnly)
Step 7: Verify the browser has a Set-Cookie header with 'refresh_token' (httpOnly)

description: Login form surfaces 401 error as a React Hook Form root error beneath the form
steps: Step 1: Register a user with known credentials
Step 2: Navigate to /login
Step 3: Enter correct email and incorrect password 'wrongpassword'
Step 4: Click 'Sign in'
Step 5: Verify the API returns 401
Step 6: Verify React Hook Form renders a root error: 'Incorrect email or password' (set via setError('root', ...))

description: GET /api/auth/me returns current user when authenticated
steps: Step 1: Log in as a valid user
Step 2: Send GET to /api/auth/me with valid access_token cookie
Step 3: Verify 200 response with user id, email, username, display_name fields

description: Access token refresh works transparently
steps: Step 1: Log in to get valid refresh_token cookie
Step 2: Expire (or remove) the access_token cookie
Step 3: Make an authenticated request to /api/auth/me
Step 4: Verify the server uses the refresh token to issue a new access_token
Step 5: Verify the response returns 200 (not 401)

description: Logout clears cookies and invalidates refresh token
steps: Step 1: Log in as a user
Step 2: POST to /api/auth/logout
Step 3: Verify response clears Set-Cookie for access_token and refresh_token
Step 4: Try using the old refresh token to refresh — verify 401

description: Unauthenticated users are redirected to /login from protected routes
steps: Step 1: Without logging in, navigate to /w/any-workspace
Step 2: Verify the browser redirects to /login
Step 3: Verify the login page is shown

description: Authenticated user is redirected away from /login back to their workspace
steps: Step 1: Log in as a user who is a member of a workspace
Step 2: Navigate to /login while already authenticated
Step 3: Verify the browser redirects to /w/:slug automatically

description: User can create a new workspace with name and emoji icon
steps: Step 1: Log in and navigate to workspace creation page
Step 2: Enter workspace name 'Test Workspace'
Step 3: Select an emoji icon
Step 4: Click 'Create workspace'
Step 5: Verify redirect to /w/test-workspace (slugified)
Step 6: Verify the workspace appears in the workspace rail on the left
Step 7: Verify a #general channel was auto-created

description: Workspace slug is auto-generated (kebab-case) from the workspace name
steps: Step 1: Create a workspace named 'My Cool Team'
Step 2: Verify the URL becomes /w/my-cool-team
Step 3: Verify POST /api/workspaces returns slug 'my-cool-team'

description: Workspace creator is assigned the 'owner' role
steps: Step 1: Log in as User A and create a workspace
Step 2: GET /api/workspaces/:slug
Step 3: Verify User A appears in the members list with role 'owner'

description: User can generate an invite link for a workspace
steps: Step 1: Log in as workspace owner
Step 2: GET /api/workspaces/:slug/invite
Step 3: Verify response contains a token string
Step 4: Verify the invite link is valid and stored in the database with an expiry 24h in the future

description: Second user can join workspace via invite link
steps: Step 1: Owner generates invite link, gets token
Step 2: Register User B with a different email
Step 3: User B POSTs to /api/workspaces/join/:token
Step 4: Verify User B is added to workspace_members with role 'member'
Step 5: Verify User B's workspace list includes the joined workspace

description: Expired invite link returns 410 Gone
steps: Step 1: Generate an invite link
Step 2: Manually set its expires_at to a past timestamp in the database
Step 3: POST to /api/workspaces/join/:token
Step 4: Verify response is 410 with error message 'Invite link expired'

description: Workspace switcher shows all workspaces the user belongs to
steps: Step 1: Log in as a user who belongs to 3 workspaces
Step 2: Verify the workspace rail on the left shows 3 workspace icons
Step 3: Click a different workspace icon and verify the URL and channel sidebar update

description: User can create a public channel in a workspace
steps: Step 1: Log in and navigate to a workspace
Step 2: Click the '+' button next to Channels in the sidebar
Step 3: Enter channel name 'general-discussion', leave public selected
Step 4: Click 'Create channel'
Step 5: Verify the channel appears in the sidebar
Step 6: Verify GET /api/workspaces/:slug/channels returns the new channel

description: Channel name field (React Hook Form + Zod) auto-normalizes and validates kebab-case format
steps: Step 1: Attempt to create channel named 'My Channel Name'
Step 2: Verify the React Hook Form field either auto-transforms the value to 'my-channel-name' or renders a Zod validation error beneath the input
Step 3: Verify the API rejects names with spaces or uppercase letters with 400 status

description: User can browse and join public channels they are not in
steps: Step 1: Create a workspace with channels #general, #announcements, #dev
Step 2: Log in as User B who is only in #general
Step 3: Open the channel browser
Step 4: Verify #announcements and #dev appear in the list
Step 5: Click 'Join' on #announcements
Step 6: Verify #announcements appears in User B's channel sidebar

description: Private channels do not appear in the channel browser
steps: Step 1: Create a private channel named 'secret-ops'
Step 2: Log in as another workspace member
Step 3: Open channel browser
Step 4: Verify 'secret-ops' does not appear in the list

description: User can send a text message to a channel
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a channel as an authenticated user
Step 2: Click the message composer
Step 3: Type 'Hello world!'
Step 4: Press Enter
Step 5: Verify the message appears in the messages list
Step 6: Verify POST /api/messages returns 201 with message data
Step 7: Verify the message is stored in the database

description: Messages appear in real time for another connected user in the same channel
steps: Step 1: Open two browser sessions — User A and User B both in the same channel
Step 2: User A types and sends 'Real-time test'
Step 3: Verify the message appears in User B's view without page refresh
Step 4: Verify the message appears within 500ms

description: Typing indicator appears for other users when someone is composing a message
steps: Step 1: Open two browser sessions in the same channel
Step 2: User A starts typing in the composer
Step 3: Verify a typing indicator appears for User B ('User A is typing...')
Step 4: User A stops typing for 3 seconds
Step 5: Verify the typing indicator disappears

description: Infinite scroll message pagination uses nuqs cursor param in URL and TanStack Query useInfiniteQuery
steps: Step 1: Send 120 messages in a channel
Step 2: Open the channel — verify the initial 50 most recent messages load
Step 3: Scroll to the top of the message list; verify nuqs updates the 'before' cursor param in the URL to the oldest visible message ID
Step 4: Verify TanStack Query fetches the next page using the nuqs 'before' cursor value from the URL
Step 5: Scroll to top again — verify the final 20 messages load
Step 6: Verify no additional loading spinner appears at the top (all messages loaded)

description: Day separator dividers appear between messages from different calendar days
steps: Step 1: Seed the database with messages on two different dates (e.g., Feb 22 and Feb 23)
Step 2: Open the channel
Step 3: Verify a divider with the date label appears between the two day groups

description: Message author can edit their own message
steps: Step 1: Send a message as User A
Step 2: Hover over the message and click 'Edit'
Step 3: Change the text and confirm edit
Step 4: Verify the message text is updated in the UI
Step 5: Verify PATCH /api/messages/:id returns 200
Step 6: Verify the message shows an '(edited)' label

description: Non-author cannot edit another user's message
steps: Step 1: User A sends a message
Step 2: Log in as User B
Step 3: Hover over User A's message
Step 4: Verify no 'Edit' option is shown for User B
Step 5: Verify PATCH /api/messages/:id returns 403 when called as User B

description: Message author can delete their own message (soft delete)
steps: Step 1: Send a message as User A
Step 2: Hover the message, click 'Delete', confirm deletion
Step 3: Verify the message is replaced with '[Message deleted]' or removed from UI
Step 4: Verify the message's deleted_at field is set in the database (not hard deleted)

description: TanStack Query optimistic mutation shows message immediately and reconciles on server confirmation
steps: Step 1: Open the channel with network throttled to slow 3G
Step 2: Type and send a message
Step 3: Verify TanStack Query's onMutate callback has added the message optimistically to the query cache before the API responds
Step 4: Verify TanStack Query's onSuccess callback replaces the optimistic entry with the server-returned message (correct id, created_at)
Step 5: Restore network and verify the message is persisted correctly

description: User can open a DM conversation with another workspace member
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a workspace as User A
Step 2: Click the '+' in the DMs section of the sidebar
Step 3: Search for User B by username
Step 4: Select User B to open the DM
Step 5: Verify a DM conversation opens (URL updates to /w/:slug/dm/:id)
Step 6: Verify POST /api/workspaces/:slug/dms returns the conversation object

description: DM messages are private between the two participants only
steps: Step 1: User A sends a DM to User B: 'Private message'
Step 2: Log in as User C who is in the same workspace
Step 3: Verify User C cannot access the DM conversation via the API (403 or 404)
Step 4: Verify the DM does not appear anywhere in User C's UI

description: DM appears in the sidebar after the first message is sent
steps: Step 1: User A opens a DM with User B
Step 2: User A sends 'Hi there!'
Step 3: Verify the DM appears in the DMs section of User A's sidebar
Step 4: Verify User B also sees the DM in their sidebar

description: Unread count badge appears on a channel when new messages arrive
steps: Step 1: User A is in workspace but has #dev channel out of focus
Step 2: User B sends 3 messages to #dev
Step 3: Verify User A sees a bold '#dev' with a badge or dot in the sidebar
Step 4: User A clicks #dev
Step 5: Verify the unread indicator disappears after opening the channel

description: Thread reply opens a thread panel and posts to the correct parent
steps: Step 1: Send a message in a channel
Step 2: Hover over the message and click 'Reply in thread'
Step 3: Verify a thread panel slides in from the right
Step 4: Verify the parent message is shown at the top of the thread panel
Step 5: Type a reply and press Enter
Step 6: Verify the reply appears in the thread panel
Step 7: Verify the reply does NOT appear in the main channel message list
Step 8: Verify the parent message shows '1 reply' below it in the channel

description: Thread replies are returned by GET /api/messages/:id/thread
steps: Step 1: Post 3 replies to a thread parent message
Step 2: GET /api/messages/:id/thread
Step 3: Verify response contains all 3 replies in chronological order
Step 4: Verify replies have thread_parent_id set to the parent message id

description: User can update their display name and status
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Profile settings
Step 2: Change display name to 'New Name'
Step 3: Add status emoji '🚀' and status text 'Shipping features'
Step 4: Save changes
Step 5: Verify PATCH /api/users/me returns updated data
Step 6: Verify the new display name appears next to messages sent by the user

description: User can upload an avatar image
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Profile settings
Step 2: Click the avatar upload area
Step 3: Select a small PNG or JPG image file
Step 4: Verify the avatar preview updates
Step 5: Save and verify the avatar appears in the workspace sidebar and on messages

description: Profile popover appears when clicking a user's avatar or name
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a channel with multiple users
Step 2: Click on any user's avatar in a message
Step 3: Verify a popover opens showing username, display name, status, and avatar
Step 4: Verify the popover closes when clicking outside it

description: User can change their password from settings
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Password settings page
Step 2: Enter current password correctly
Step 3: Enter new password 'NewPass456!'
Step 4: Confirm new password
Step 5: Save
Step 6: Log out and log in with the new password
Step 7: Verify login succeeds

description: Presence indicator shows green dot for online users
steps: Step 1: User A and User B are both connected to the WebSocket
Step 2: Navigate to a channel where User B has sent a message
Step 3: Verify User B's avatar has a green dot overlay
Step 4: User B closes their browser (disconnects)
Step 5: Verify the green dot disappears from User B's avatar within 5 seconds

description: Browser tab title shows unread count when the tab is not focused
steps: Step 1: Open app in a browser tab and switch focus to another tab
Step 2: Have another user send 3 messages to the current channel
Step 3: Verify the browser tab title changes to '(3) Colloquium Chat'
Step 4: Switch back to the app tab
Step 5: Verify the title resets to 'Colloquium Chat'

description: @mention highlights the username in the message text
steps: Step 1: User A sends message 'Hey @testuser check this out'
Step 2: Verify the '@testuser' portion of the rendered message is visually highlighted
Step 3: Verify the message appears in a special notification section for testuser

description: Workspace owner can archive a channel
steps: Step 1: Log in as workspace owner
Step 2: Open channel settings for #dev
Step 3: Click 'Archive channel'
Step 4: Confirm the action
Step 5: Verify the channel no longer appears in the active channel sidebar
Step 6: Verify is_archived=1 in the database

description: WebSocket reconnects automatically after connection drop
steps: Step 1: Open the app and connect WebSocket
Step 2: Simulate network disconnect (DevTools → offline for 2 seconds)
Step 3: Restore network
Step 4: Verify the app reconnects (exponential backoff attempts are visible in console)
Step 5: Verify messages continue to be received after reconnect

description: Markdown renders bold, italic, and inline code in messages
steps: Step 1: Send a message containing '**bold** _italic_ `code`'
Step 2: Verify the rendered message shows bold text, italic text, and monospace code
Step 3: Verify the raw Markdown syntax characters are not visible in the rendered message

description: Fenced code blocks render with syntax highlighting
steps: Step 1: Send a message with a code block: ```javascript\nconst x = 1;\n```
Step 2: Verify the code block is rendered with a dark background
Step 3: Verify JavaScript syntax tokens are color-highlighted (keywords, strings different colors)

description: Message composer enforces 4000-character Zod limit and disables submit via React Hook Form
steps: Step 1: In the composer, paste text of exactly 4001 characters
Step 2: Verify the Zod schema validation triggers a React Hook Form error when content exceeds 4000 characters
Step 3: Verify the Send button is disabled because React Hook Form formState.isValid is false when the Zod max() constraint is violated
Step 4: Verify a character counter shows the count, with warning styling near the limit

description: Shift+Enter inserts a newline in the composer without sending
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a channel and click the composer
Step 2: Type 'Line one'
Step 3: Press Shift+Enter
Step 4: Type 'Line two'
Step 5: Verify the composer shows two lines without sending the message
Step 6: Press Enter to send
Step 7: Verify the sent message contains a line break between the two lines

description: Emoji picker inserts emoji into the composer at cursor position
steps: Step 1: Click the emoji icon in the composer toolbar
Step 2: Verify an emoji picker popover opens
Step 3: Click on an emoji (e.g., 😊)
Step 4: Verify the emoji is inserted into the composer text at the current cursor position
Step 5: Verify the picker closes after selection

description: API returns consistent error shape for all 4xx responses
steps: Step 1: Trigger a 404 error (GET /api/nonexistent)
Step 2: Verify response JSON has { error: string, code: string } shape
Step 3: Trigger a 422 error (POST /api/auth/register with missing fields)
Step 4: Verify same error shape with validation details

description: SQLite WAL mode is enabled for the database
steps: Step 1: Start the API server
Step 2: Query the database with PRAGMA journal_mode
Step 3: Verify the result is 'wal'

description: Workspace settings page allows owner to rename the workspace
steps: Step 1: Navigate to workspace settings as the owner
Step 2: Change workspace name to 'Renamed Team'
Step 3: Save
Step 4: Verify PATCH /api/workspaces/:slug succeeds
Step 5: Verify the new name appears in the workspace rail tooltip and sidebar header

description: Non-owner members cannot access workspace settings write operations
steps: Step 1: Log in as a 'member' role user in a workspace
Step 2: PATCH /api/workspaces/:slug with a name change
Step 3: Verify response is 403 Forbidden
Step 4: Verify workspace name is unchanged in the database

description: Members list (TanStack Table) renders all rows with correct role and online status columns
steps: Step 1: Have 3 users in a workspace (1 owner, 1 admin, 1 member)
Step 2: Navigate to the workspace members list
Step 3: Verify TanStack Table renders 3 data rows, each with role cell content matching the user's actual role (owner/admin/member)
Step 4: Verify online users have a green indicator in the online status column cell via TanStack Table's cell renderer

description: Turbo build succeeds for both frontend and API packages
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm turbo build --filter=colloquium-chat... --filter=colloquium-chat-api...`
Step 2: Verify exit code is 0
Step 3: Verify apps/colloquium-chat/dist directory is created
Step 4: Verify apps/colloquium-chat-api/dist directory is created

description: TypeScript strict mode passes with zero errors across both packages
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=colloquium-chat... --filter=colloquium-chat-api...`
Step 2: Verify exit code is 0
Step 3: Verify no 'any' types without explanatory comments in the output

description: Frontend Vite dev server starts on port 5173
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm dev` in apps/colloquium-chat
Step 2: Verify server output shows 'Local: http://localhost:5173'
Step 3: Verify navigating to http://localhost:5173 renders the app

description: API server starts on port 5001
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm dev` in apps/colloquium-chat-api
Step 2: Verify server output shows port 5001
Step 3: Verify GET http://localhost:5001/api/health returns 200

description: Channel header shows channel name, description, and member count
steps: Step 1: Create a channel with description 'For dev discussions'
Step 2: Navigate to the channel
Step 3: Verify the header shows '#channel-name'
Step 4: Verify the description 'For dev discussions' is visible in the header
Step 5: Verify a member count chip shows the correct number of members

description: GET /api/workspaces/:slug/channels/browse only returns public non-archived channels
steps: Step 1: Create workspace with: #public-one (public), #secret (private), #old (archived)
Step 2: GET /api/workspaces/:slug/channels/browse
Step 3: Verify #public-one appears
Step 4: Verify #secret does NOT appear
Step 5: Verify #old does NOT appear

description: Drizzle ORM migrations run successfully on fresh database
steps: Step 1: Delete the SQLite database file
Step 2: Run `pnpm db:migrate` in colloquium-chat-api
Step 3: Verify all tables are created: users, workspaces, channels, messages, etc.
Step 4: Verify the exit code is 0

description: Message timestamps are relative for recent messages and absolute for older ones
steps: Step 1: Send a message (timestamp should show e.g. '2 min ago')
Step 2: Seed a message with a timestamp 2 days ago
Step 3: Verify recent message shows relative time
Step 4: Verify old message shows an absolute date-time format (e.g., 'Feb 21, 2026 at 3:45 PM')

description: Empty/whitespace message rejected by Zod .trim().min(1) on client (React Hook Form) and API
steps: Step 1: Focus the composer and type only spaces
Step 2: Verify the Send button is disabled because the Zod schema's .trim().min(1) rule marks the field invalid in React Hook Form
Step 3: POST /api/messages with content '   '
Step 4: Verify response is 422 Unprocessable Entity

description: Thread reply count updates in real time for all channel viewers
steps: Step 1: User A has a channel open and sees a message with 0 replies
Step 2: User B opens the thread and posts a reply
Step 3: Verify User A sees the reply count update to '1 reply' without refreshing

description: DM unread count appears in sidebar when receiving a new DM while on another view
steps: Step 1: User A is viewing a channel (not their DM with User B)
Step 2: User B sends User A a DM
Step 3: Verify User A's DM list in the sidebar shows User B's name bold with unread indicator

description: pnpm install completes without errors in the monorepo
steps: Step 1: Run `pnpm install` from the repository root
Step 2: Verify exit code is 0
Step 3: Verify node_modules are created in apps/colloquium-chat and apps/colloquium-chat-api
Step 4: Verify no peer dependency warnings that would break the build

description: User can leave a workspace from workspace settings
steps: Step 1: Log in as a 'member' user in a workspace
Step 2: Navigate to workspace settings → danger zone
Step 3: Click 'Leave workspace' and confirm
Step 4: Verify redirect to a workspace selection page or /w/new
Step 5: Verify the workspace no longer appears in the user's workspace rail

description: Owner cannot leave workspace if they are the last member
steps: Step 1: Create a workspace as User A with no other members
Step 2: Attempt to leave the workspace
Step 3: Verify an error message: 'Transfer ownership before leaving' or similar
Step 4: Verify the user remains in the workspace

description: Messages API paginates with before cursor; frontend passes cursor via nuqs search param
steps: Step 1: Send 60 messages in a channel
Step 2: GET /api/messages?channelId=X&limit=50 — verify 50 newest messages returned
Step 3: Verify nuqs serializes the cursor into the URL as ?before=:id and TanStack Query key includes this param for cache segmentation
Step 4: Verify 10 remaining messages are returned
Step 5: Another call with before cursor returns empty array (hasMore: false)

description: WebSocket sends user.typing event throttled to at most once per 500ms
steps: Step 1: Monitor WebSocket frames in DevTools
Step 2: Type rapidly in the composer (10 keystrokes in 1 second)
Step 3: Verify no more than 3 user.typing frames are sent during that 1 second
Step 4: Verify a user.typing.stop or silence after 3s removes typing indicator on other clients

description: Frontend route /w/:slug/channel/:name correctly loads the channel
steps: Step 1: Navigate directly to /w/my-workspace/channel/general
Step 2: Verify the channel header shows '#general'
Step 3: Verify messages load
Step 4: Verify the channel is highlighted in the sidebar

description: Attempting to access a workspace you are not a member of returns 403
steps: Step 1: Create Workspace A as User A
Step 2: Log in as User B who has no membership in Workspace A
Step 3: GET /api/workspaces/workspace-a-slug
Step 4: Verify response is 403 Forbidden

description: Zustand store correctly persists active workspace and channel selection across navigation
steps: Step 1: Navigate to workspace 'team-a' and channel '#dev'
Step 2: Navigate to workspace 'team-b' (switching workspaces)
Step 3: Navigate back to workspace 'team-a'
Step 4: Verify the previously active channel '#dev' is restored for 'team-a'

description: Invite link works only once when max_uses is set to 1
steps: Step 1: Generate an invite link with max_uses=1
Step 2: User B uses the link to join
Step 3: User C attempts to use the same link
Step 4: Verify User C gets a 410 Gone or 400 response indicating the link is exhausted

description: Page refresh restores active workspace and channel because route params and nuqs URL state are authoritative
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /w/my-workspace/channel/dev
Step 2: Refresh the browser
Step 3: Verify the route params and any nuqs search params survive the refresh and drive the same TanStack Query data fetch
Step 4: Verify the user does not have to re-authenticate

description: WebSocket connection authenticates using the access_token cookie
steps: Step 1: Connect to WS /ws without a valid token
Step 2: Verify the server closes the connection with code 4001 (Unauthorized)
Step 3: Connect with a valid access_token
Step 4: Verify the connection remains open and receives a welcome/ack event

description: Soft-deleted messages show placeholder text, not raw content
steps: Step 1: Author deletes a message
Step 2: Other users viewing the channel see '[This message was deleted]' in place of the content
Step 3: Verify the original content field in the database has deleted_at set but content preserved server-side

description: Channel members count decrements when a user leaves the workspace
steps: Step 1: Workspace has User A and User B, both members of #general (count: 2)
Step 2: User B leaves the workspace
Step 3: Navigate to #general as User A
Step 4: Verify member count in channel header shows 1

description: App displays a 404 page for unknown routes
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /some/nonexistent/route
Step 2: Verify a 404 or 'Page not found' component renders
Step 3: Verify the page includes a link to go back to the home/workspace page

description: Workspace icon in rail shows emoji or initials avatar when no custom image
steps: Step 1: Create workspace 'Alpha Corp' with emoji icon '🚀'
Step 2: Create workspace 'Beta Inc' with no icon
Step 3: Verify 'Alpha Corp' workspace icon shows '🚀'
Step 4: Verify 'Beta Inc' workspace icon shows 'B' as an initials avatar

description: Message edit history: edited_at timestamp is set on edit
steps: Step 1: Send a message
Step 2: Edit the message
Step 3: GET /api/messages?channelId=X
Step 4: Verify the edited message has edited_at set to a non-null timestamp

description: Admin can delete other members' messages in their workspace
steps: Step 1: User A is workspace admin; User B sends a message
Step 2: User A (admin) calls DELETE /api/messages/:id on User B's message
Step 3: Verify response is 200 and deleted_at is set

description: Channel description can be updated by admins
steps: Step 1: Log in as workspace admin
Step 2: PATCH /api/workspaces/:slug/channels/:name with { description: 'New description' }
Step 3: Verify response is 200
Step 4: Navigate to the channel — verify the header shows 'New description'

description: GET /api/workspaces returns only workspaces the current user belongs to
steps: Step 1: User A is in Workspace 1 and Workspace 2
Step 2: User B is only in Workspace 1
Step 3: GET /api/workspaces as User B
Step 4: Verify only Workspace 1 is returned (not Workspace 2)

description: Composer renders markdown preview toggle correctly
steps: Step 1: Type '**Hello** _world_' in the composer
Step 2: Click the preview toggle button
Step 3: Verify the composer switches to a rendered view showing bold 'Hello' and italic 'world'
Step 4: Click preview toggle again to return to edit mode
Step 5: Verify raw markdown text is editable again

description: Thread panel closes when clicking outside it or pressing Escape
steps: Step 1: Open a thread panel
Step 2: Press Escape key
Step 3: Verify the thread panel closes
Step 4: Open thread panel again
Step 5: Click on the main chat area outside the panel
Step 6: Verify the panel closes

description: Channel sidebar sections (Channels, DMs) can be collapsed and expanded
steps: Step 1: Click the 'Channels' section header to collapse it
Step 2: Verify the channel list is hidden
Step 3: Click again to expand
Step 4: Verify channels reappear
Step 5: Verify collapse state is preserved after navigating to a different view

description: App renders with a dark background (#0d0d0d) on all main areas
steps: Step 1: Open the app in a browser
Step 2: Take a screenshot of the full-page layout
Step 3: Verify the body/main background color is #0d0d0d or equivalent near-black

description: Workspace rail column has surface background (#161616) and correct width (~64px)
steps: Step 1: Open the app with at least one workspace
Step 2: Inspect the workspace rail element
Step 3: Verify background-color is #161616
Step 4: Verify computed width is approximately 64px

description: Channel sidebar has surface background and correct width (~240px)
steps: Step 1: Open a workspace
Step 2: Inspect the channel sidebar
Step 3: Verify background-color is #161616
Step 4: Verify computed width is approximately 240px

description: Active workspace icon in rail shows a white ring/border highlight
steps: Step 1: Open a workspace so it is active
Step 2: Inspect the active workspace icon in the rail
Step 3: Verify a visible ring or border using the accent color (#ffffff) surrounds the icon

description: Active channel in sidebar shows left accent border and elevated background
steps: Step 1: Click on a channel to make it active
Step 2: Inspect the active channel row in the sidebar
Step 3: Verify a left border in accent color (#ffffff) is visible
Step 4: Verify the row background is Surface2 (#1e1e1e)

description: Unread channel shows bold name and indigo (#5865f2) unread dot
steps: Step 1: Trigger unread state on a channel
Step 2: Inspect the channel row in the sidebar
Step 3: Verify channel name font-weight is 600 or bold
Step 4: Verify an 8px circular badge with color #5865f2 is rendered

description: Message rows have correct 12px vertical padding and 36px avatar
steps: Step 1: Send a message and view the channel
Step 2: Inspect a message row element
Step 3: Verify padding-top and padding-bottom are 12px
Step 4: Verify the avatar element inside the row is 36×36px

description: Composer box has rounded corners, border, and correct background
steps: Step 1: Open a channel and inspect the message composer
Step 2: Verify border-radius is at least 8px (rounded-lg equivalent)
Step 3: Verify border is 1px solid #2a2a2a
Step 4: Verify background-color is #161616 (Surface)

description: Inter font is loaded and applied to body text
steps: Step 1: Open the app
Step 2: Inspect the body or main content element
Step 3: Verify font-family includes 'Inter' as the first choice
Step 4: Verify the font is actually loaded (check network requests for an Inter font file)

description: Code blocks use JetBrains Mono font at 13px
steps: Step 1: Send a message with a fenced code block
Step 2: Inspect the rendered code block element
Step 3: Verify font-family includes 'JetBrains Mono'
Step 4: Verify font-size is 13px

description: Primary text color is #e8e8e8 (not pure white) for body content
steps: Step 1: Send a message and inspect the message content element
Step 2: Verify color is #e8e8e8
Step 3: Inspect a sidebar channel name
Step 4: Verify channel name uses #e8e8e8 as well

description: Muted text (timestamps, placeholders) uses #6b6b6b
steps: Step 1: Send a message and inspect the timestamp element
Step 2: Verify color is #6b6b6b
Step 3: Inspect the composer placeholder text
Step 4: Verify placeholder color is #6b6b6b

description: Login / register page shows centered card on dark background
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /login
Step 2: Take a screenshot
Step 3: Verify the form is centered horizontally and vertically
Step 4: Verify the background behind the card is #0d0d0d
Step 5: Verify the card itself has a Surface (#161616) or Surface2 (#1e1e1e) background

description: Buttons use correct variant styles — primary (white bg, black text) and ghost
steps: Step 1: Locate a primary action button (e.g., 'Sign in' or 'Create workspace')
Step 2: Verify background-color is #ffffff and color is #000000
Step 3: Locate a ghost button (e.g., 'Cancel')
Step 4: Verify the ghost button has a transparent background and shows a Surface2 bg on hover

description: Danger buttons (Delete, Leave) use #ef4444 red color
steps: Step 1: Navigate to workspace settings danger zone
Step 2: Inspect the 'Leave workspace' or 'Delete workspace' button
Step 3: Verify background-color is #ef4444
Step 4: Verify the button text is white for contrast

description: Avatar fallback shows initials on Surface2 background when no image
steps: Step 1: Log in as a user with no avatar uploaded
Step 2: View any message sent by this user
Step 3: Verify the avatar circle shows the user's initials (e.g., 'AT' for Ali Taccebeci)
Step 4: Verify the avatar background is #1e1e1e (Surface2)
Step 5: Verify the initials text is white/light

description: Online presence dot is green, 10px, and positioned bottom-right of avatar
steps: Step 1: Have a user online (connected)
Step 2: View their avatar in the channel or sidebar
Step 3: Verify a circular green dot (approx. 10px) is visible at the bottom-right corner of their avatar

description: Message hover reveals action buttons without layout shift
steps: Step 1: Hover over a message in the channel
Step 2: Verify action icons (reply, edit, delete) appear in a floating bar above the message
Step 3: Verify no surrounding messages shift vertically when hover state activates
Step 4: Verify the action bar has a Surface (#161616) background and border

description: Day separator divider has centered date label on a horizontal line
steps: Step 1: View a channel with messages spanning multiple days
Step 2: Verify a horizontal rule divider is visible between message groups
Step 3: Verify a date label (e.g., 'February 22, 2026') is centered on or above the line
Step 4: Verify the divider text is in muted color #6b6b6b

description: New message fade-in animation plays when a message arrives
steps: Step 1: Open a channel with another user
Step 2: Have User B send a message
Step 3: Verify the new message element has a brief fade-in animation (120ms ease-out)
Step 4: Verify the animation does not cause a flash of unstyled content

description: Thread panel slides in from the right with a 200ms ease-out transition
steps: Step 1: Open a thread from a message
Step 2: Verify the panel animates in from the right edge
Step 3: Verify the animation duration is approximately 200ms
Step 4: Verify the main chat area compresses or overlays cleanly

description: Typing indicator shows animated bouncing dots
steps: Step 1: Have User B start typing in a channel
Step 2: As User A, observe the typing indicator area above the composer
Step 3: Verify an animated element with 2-3 dots is visible
Step 4: Verify the dots bounce or pulse in a looping animation

description: Markdown bold/italic/code renders with visually distinct styling
steps: Step 1: Send a message with **bold** _italic_ and `inline code`
Step 2: Verify bold text has higher font-weight (700)
Step 3: Verify italic text has font-style: italic
Step 4: Verify inline code has a monospace font and a subtle background (e.g., Surface2)

description: @mention text is highlighted in the accent/indigo color
steps: Step 1: Send a message containing '@testuser'
Step 2: Verify '@testuser' is rendered with a distinct color or background highlight
Step 3: Verify the highlight uses the Unread/accent color (#5865f2)

description: Workspace name and sidebar header typography is correct
steps: Step 1: Open a workspace
Step 2: Inspect the workspace name in the sidebar header
Step 3: Verify font-weight is semi-bold (600) or bold
Step 4: Verify font-size is 16px

description: Sidebar section labels (CHANNELS, DIRECT MESSAGES) are 13px medium weight
steps: Step 1: Open a workspace and inspect the 'Channels' section label
Step 2: Verify font-size is 13px
Step 3: Verify font-weight is 500 (medium)
Step 4: Verify the label text is in muted color #6b6b6b

description: Profile popover has correct surface background and border
steps: Step 1: Click a user's avatar to open the profile popover
Step 2: Inspect the popover element
Step 3: Verify background is #161616 (Surface)
Step 4: Verify there is a 1px border in #2a2a2a
Step 5: Verify the popover has border-radius >= 8px

description: Reply count badge on parent message is visually distinct (indigo text/color)
steps: Step 1: Post replies in a thread
Step 2: View the parent message in the channel
Step 3: Verify a '2 replies' link/badge is visible below the message content
Step 4: Verify the text or icon uses the indigo accent color #5865f2

description: Empty channel state shows a welcome/empty state illustration and text
steps: Step 1: Create a new channel with no messages
Step 2: Navigate to the channel
Step 3: Verify an empty state UI is shown (not a blank white space)
Step 4: Verify text says something like 'This is the beginning of #channel-name'
Step 5: Verify an icon or simple illustration accompanies the text

description: Skeleton loaders appear while TanStack Query isLoading is true for the messages query
steps: Step 1: Slow down network (3G throttle)
Step 2: Navigate to a channel
Step 3: Verify the component renders skeleton rows when TanStack Query's useInfiniteQuery returns isLoading: true (no cached data)
Step 4: Verify the skeletons use a subtle animated shimmer effect
Step 5: Verify skeletons are replaced by real message rows when TanStack Query transitions to isSuccess: true

description: Mobile layout hides the sidebar behind a hamburger menu
steps: Step 1: Resize the browser viewport to 375px width (iPhone-size)
Step 2: Verify the channel sidebar and workspace rail are hidden
Step 3: Verify a hamburger menu icon is visible in the header
Step 4: Click the hamburger
Step 5: Verify the sidebar slides in as a drawer overlay

description: Mobile composer is touch-friendly with adequate tap target size (≥44px height)
steps: Step 1: Set viewport to 375px width
Step 2: View the message composer
Step 3: Verify the composer input area is at least 44px tall
Step 4: Verify the send button has a tap target of at least 44×44px

description: Settings page uses a sidebar nav + right content panel layout
steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings
Step 2: Verify a left navigation panel shows settings categories (Profile, Password, Workspace)
Step 3: Verify the right panel shows the content for the active settings category
Step 4: Verify clicking a different category updates the right panel without full page reload

description: Workspace settings danger zone is visually separated from other settings
steps: Step 1: Navigate to workspace settings
Step 2: Scroll to the danger zone section
Step 3: Verify it is visually separated (e.g., red border or red section header 'Danger Zone')
Step 4: Verify the separator makes the section stand out as destructive

description: Invite link display has a copy-to-clipboard button with visual feedback
steps: Step 1: Generate an invite link in workspace settings
Step 2: Verify the link is shown in a read-only input or code block
Step 3: Verify a 'Copy' button is adjacent to the link
Step 4: Click 'Copy' and verify it changes to 'Copied!' with a brief animation

description: Emoji picker is styled consistently with the dark theme
steps: Step 1: Click the emoji icon in the composer to open the picker
Step 2: Verify the picker background is #161616 or #1e1e1e (not white)
Step 3: Verify emoji cells have hover states in Surface2 color
Step 4: Verify the picker has a border matching #2a2a2a

description: Channel browser modal/page has correct dark styling
steps: Step 1: Open the channel browser
Step 2: Verify the background is dark (#0d0d0d or #161616)
Step 3: Verify each channel row has hover state in Surface2
Step 4: Verify the 'Join' button uses the primary style (white bg)

description: Focus rings are visible on all interactive elements for keyboard accessibility
steps: Step 1: Tab through the UI elements on the login page
Step 2: Verify each focused element shows a visible focus ring
Step 3: Verify focus ring uses the accent color (#ffffff) outline
Step 4: Tab through the sidebar channel list and verify focus rings on each channel row

description: No white background bleeds through on any dark theme component
steps: Step 1: Open the app and navigate through all main views (channel, DM, settings, profile)
Step 2: Take screenshots of each view
Step 3: Verify no component shows a white or near-white (#f0f0f0+) background
Step 4: Verify all dialogs, modals, popovers, and dropdowns use dark surfaces

description: Member role badges (owner, admin, member) are visually distinguishable
steps: Step 1: View the workspace members list with users of different roles
Step 2: Verify 'owner' has a distinct badge/label (e.g., outlined in accent)
Step 3: Verify 'admin' has a different badge from 'member'
Step 4: Verify all badges are legible on the dark surface background

description: Thread panel parent message is visually distinct from replies
steps: Step 1: Open a thread panel
Step 2: Verify the parent message at the top is visually separated from replies (e.g., by a divider or background)
Step 3: Verify replies have slightly different indentation or visual treatment

description: Loading spinner/indicator is shown while performing authentication actions
steps: Step 1: Slow down the network
Step 2: Click 'Sign in' on the login page
Step 3: Verify a loading indicator (spinner or skeleton) appears on the button or form
Step 4: Verify the submit button is disabled while loading

description: Workspace creation form is visually clean and matches the dark theme
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the workspace creation page (/w/new)
Step 2: Verify the form has dark background inputs with #2a2a2a border
Step 3: Verify input focus state uses the accent (#ffffff) ring
Step 4: Verify the emoji picker for workspace icon is inline and matches the dark theme

description: Color transitions for hover/active states are 100ms ease
steps: Step 1: Inspect a channel row in the sidebar via DevTools
Step 2: Verify transition property includes 'background-color 100ms ease' or 'all 100ms ease'
Step 3: Hover the element and verify the state change is visually smooth

description: Edited message shows '(edited)' label in muted color
steps: Step 1: Edit a previously sent message
Step 2: Verify the message now shows '(edited)' appended after the content
Step 3: Verify '(edited)' is in muted text color #6b6b6b
Step 4: Verify the label is smaller than the message text

description: DM header shows recipient avatar, name, and online status clearly
steps: Step 1: Open a DM conversation
Step 2: Verify the header shows the recipient's avatar (36px or larger)
Step 3: Verify the display name or username is shown in the header
Step 4: Verify the online status indicator (green dot or 'Active now' text) is visible

description: User status emoji and text display next to username in profile popover
steps: Step 1: Set a status '🚀 Shipping features' for a user
Step 2: Click that user's avatar to open the profile popover
Step 3: Verify the status emoji and text 'Shipping features' are visible in the popover
Step 4: Verify the status is below or beside the display name

description: Character counter in composer turns warning color near the 4000 char limit
steps: Step 1: Type 3800 characters in the composer
Step 2: Verify a character counter is visible (e.g., '3800 / 4000')
Step 3: Verify the counter text color is warning/amber when above 3500
Step 4: Type to 4000 characters and verify counter turns danger red (#ef4444)

description: Channel sidebar + workspace rail maintain layout on small laptop screens (1280px wide)
steps: Step 1: Set viewport to 1280×800
Step 2: Verify all three columns (workspace rail, sidebar, chat main) are visible
Step 3: Verify no horizontal overflow or cut-off elements
Step 4: Verify the chat main area has adequate reading width (at least 600px)

description: Unread badge on workspace icon in rail indicates unread messages in that workspace
steps: Step 1: Be in Workspace A; Workspace B receives a new message
Step 2: Verify Workspace B's icon in the rail shows a small badge/dot
Step 3: Switch to Workspace B and open the unread channel
Step 4: Verify the badge on Workspace B's icon disappears

description: All color changes and transitions are GPU-composited (no layout-triggering animations)
steps: Step 1: Open Chrome DevTools → Performance tab
Step 2: Record while sending messages and opening threads
Step 3: Verify animations use only transform and opacity (not top/left/width/height)
Step 4: Verify no layout or paint warnings in the performance trace

description: Infinite scroll loading indicator appears at the top of the messages list
steps: Step 1: Load a channel with 100+ messages
Step 2: Scroll to the top quickly
Step 3: Verify a loading spinner or skeleton rows appear at the top while older messages fetch
Step 4: Verify the scroll position is preserved after older messages are prepended

description: Register form (React Hook Form watch) drives live password strength indicator
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /register
Step 2: Type a weak password (e.g., '123') — verify React Hook Form watch() or onChange triggers the strength indicator to update without submitting
Step 3: Verify a strength indicator shows 'Weak' in red
Step 4: Type a strong password — verify the indicator updates reactively via React Hook Form field watching
Step 5: Verify the indicator shows 'Strong' in green

description: DM list in sidebar shows recipient avatar (24px) beside their name
steps: Step 1: Have 2 active DM conversations
Step 2: View the DMs section in the sidebar
Step 3: Verify each DM row shows a 24px circular avatar of the recipient
Step 4: Verify the avatar is followed by the recipient's display name

description: No-workspace state shows a prompt to create or join a workspace
steps: Step 1: Log in as a user with no workspaces
Step 2: Verify the UI shows an empty state prompt
Step 3: Verify buttons for 'Create workspace' and 'Join with invite link' are visible
Step 4: Verify the page is styled consistently with the dark theme

description: Code block in messages has a subtle copy button on hover
steps: Step 1: Send a message with a fenced code block
Step 2: Hover over the rendered code block
Step 3: Verify a 'Copy' button appears in the top-right corner of the code block
Step 4: Click it and verify the code is copied to clipboard
Step 5: Verify the button shows 'Copied!' feedback

description: Channel member is auto-added to #general when joining a workspace
steps: Step 1: User B joins workspace via invite link
Step 2: GET /api/workspaces/:slug/channels as User B
Step 3: Verify #general appears in the channel list
Step 4: Verify User B is in channel_members for #general

description: GET /api/auth/me returns 401 when called without authentication cookies
steps: Step 1: Make a GET request to /api/auth/me without any cookies
Step 2: Verify response status is 401
Step 3: Verify response body contains an error message about missing authentication

description: Channel browser (TanStack Table) displays message count column per channel row
steps: Step 1: Send 5 messages to #announcements
Step 2: Open the channel browser as a user not in #announcements
Step 3: Verify the TanStack Table row for #announcements renders a message count cell matching the database count
Step 4: Verify the number matches the actual database count

description: Workspace invite link is copyable from the invite modal in the UI
steps: Step 1: Log in as workspace owner
Step 2: Open workspace settings or click Invite People button
Step 3: Verify an invite link is displayed in the UI
Step 4: Click the copy button
Step 5: Verify clipboard contains the full invite URL
Step 6: Verify the URL format is http://localhost:5173/join/:token or similar

description: User display name defaults to username when no display name is set
steps: Step 1: Register a new user with username testuser123 and no display name
Step 2: Navigate to a channel and send a message
Step 3: Verify the message shows testuser123 as the sender name
Step 4: GET /api/auth/me and verify display_name field is null or equals username

description: Drizzle ORM query for messages uses indexed columns for performance
steps: Step 1: Inspect the Drizzle schema definition file
Step 2: Verify messages.channel_id has a database index
Step 3: Verify messages.created_at has a database index
Step 4: Verify messages.thread_parent_id has a database index

description: WebSocket room subscription correctly isolates channel messages
steps: Step 1: User A subscribes to #general channel via WebSocket
Step 2: User B subscribes to #dev channel
Step 3: User C sends a message to #dev
Step 4: Verify User B receives the message.new event
Step 5: Verify User A does NOT receive the message.new event for #dev

description: Registering with an invalid username is rejected by Zod schema on the API with a 422 response
steps: Step 1: POST /api/auth/register with username containing spaces or special chars like my user!
Step 2: Verify response is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Step 3: Verify the Zod validation error message from the API describes the allowed username pattern (e.g., alphanumeric + underscores only)
Step 4: Verify no user is created in the database

description: GET /api/workspaces/:slug/channels returns channels sorted alphabetically
steps: Step 1: Create channels: #zebra, #alpha, #middle in a workspace
Step 2: Join all channels as a user
Step 3: GET /api/workspaces/:slug/channels
Step 4: Verify channels appear in order: alpha, middle, zebra

description: DM conversation deduplication: opening DM twice returns same conversation
steps: Step 1: User A opens DM with User B (POST /api/workspaces/:slug/dms)
Step 2: User A opens DM with User B again
Step 3: Verify both calls return the same conversation id
Step 4: Verify only one row exists in direct_message_conversations table

description: Message content_rendered field is stored as sanitized HTML
steps: Step 1: Send a message with Markdown including a potential XSS payload: **bold** <script>alert(1)</script>
Step 2: Verify the message renders bold but does not execute the script
Step 3: Check database: content_rendered should have <script> tags stripped or escaped
Step 4: Verify browser does not show any alert dialog

description: Logout button in user menu clears session and redirects to login
steps: Step 1: Log in and navigate to a workspace
Step 2: Click on the user avatar or account menu in the workspace rail
Step 3: Click Logout
Step 4: Verify redirect to /login
Step 5: Verify cookies are cleared (no access_token or refresh_token in browser)
Step 6: Attempting to navigate to /w/:slug redirects back to /login

description: Workspace creation page emoji picker for icon is consistent with dark theme
steps: Step 1: Navigate to workspace creation
Step 2: Click the emoji icon picker area
Step 3: Verify the emoji grid appears with a dark background
Step 4: Verify currently selected emoji has a highlighted state

description: Channel header border-bottom separates it from the message list
steps: Step 1: Open a channel
Step 2: Inspect the channel header element
Step 3: Verify a border-bottom of 1px in color #2a2a2a separates the header from messages
Step 4: Verify no gap or spacing issue at the border line

description: Composer toolbar icons have correct size and spacing
steps: Step 1: Open a channel composer
Step 2: Inspect the toolbar area (bold, code, emoji icons)
Step 3: Verify icons are 20x20px or similar consistent size
Step 4: Verify icons have 8px gap between them
Step 5: Verify icon color is #6b6b6b (muted) and brightens to #e8e8e8 on hover

description: Workspace rail add button (+) has circular style matching workspace icons
steps: Step 1: Open the app with at least one workspace
Step 2: View the bottom of the workspace rail
Step 3: Verify the Add Workspace (+) button is circular (same shape as workspace avatars)
Step 4: Verify it uses a dashed border or subdued style to distinguish from active workspaces

description: Message composer placeholder text is descriptive and correctly styled
steps: Step 1: Open a channel with an empty composer
Step 2: Verify placeholder text reads something like Message #channel-name
Step 3: Verify placeholder color is #6b6b6b
Step 4: Verify placeholder disappears when user clicks into the composer

description: Channel row in sidebar shows # prefix before channel name
steps: Step 1: Open a workspace with several channels
Step 2: View the channels section in the sidebar
Step 3: Verify each channel name is prefixed with # symbol
Step 4: Verify the # is in muted color and the name is in primary text color

description: Message list area is scrollable and does not cause page-level scroll
steps: Step 1: Open a channel with many messages
Step 2: Scroll within the message list
Step 3: Verify only the message list area scrolls (not the entire page)
Step 4: Verify the sidebar and composer remain fixed in position

description: React Hook Form field error messages are styled in red (#ef4444) beneath their inputs
steps: Step 1: Submit the login form with invalid credentials and observe React Hook Form validation errors appear

Step 2: Verify React Hook Form renders the error message as a

or directly beneath the relevant field
Step 3: Verify the error text color is #ef4444
Step 4: Verify error text is legible (adequate font size, not too small)

description: Scrollbar in message list and sidebar is styled to match the dark theme
steps: Step 1: Open a channel with many messages and a workspace with many channels
Step 2: Verify the scrollbar uses a dark/muted style (thin, dark-colored thumb)
Step 3: Verify no default bright grey OS scrollbar is visible
Step 4: Verify scrollbar thumb color is approximately #2a2a2a or similar

description: User avatar in workspace rail bottom shows current user identity
steps: Step 1: Log in and open a workspace
Step 2: View the bottom of the workspace rail
Step 3: Verify the current users avatar (or initials) is shown
Step 4: Verify clicking it opens a menu with options: Profile, Settings, Logout

description: Workspace member count and channel list are visible in workspace header dropdown
steps: Step 1: Click the workspace name in the sidebar header to open the dropdown
Step 2: Verify a dropdown appears with options like Invite People, Settings, Leave Workspace
Step 3: Verify the dropdown is styled dark and matches the surface theme
Step 4: Verify the dropdown closes when clicking outside

description: Full 3-column layout is correctly proportioned on 1920px wide screen
steps: Step 1: Set viewport to 1920x1080
Step 2: Open the app with a workspace and channel
Step 3: Verify workspace rail is ~64px, sidebar ~240px, and chat main fills remaining width
Step 4: Verify the chat main column is max ~900px centered or full remaining width

description: Thread panel does not obscure the composer when open
steps: Step 1: Open a thread panel in a channel
Step 2: Verify the message composer is still fully visible and accessible
Step 3: Verify the thread panel overlays the right portion of the chat main area
Step 4: Verify typing in the composer (not thread) still works normally

description: Register page has a link to login and login page has a link to register
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /register
Step 2: Verify there is a link or text like Already have an account? Sign in
Step 3: Navigate to /login
Step 4: Verify there is a link like Dont have an account? Create one
Step 5: Verify both links correctly navigate to the other page

description: Channel name in header has # prefix matching sidebar style
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a channel
Step 2: Inspect the channel header
Step 3: Verify the channel name is displayed as #channel-name with the # symbol
Step 4: Verify consistent styling with the sidebar channel list format

description: Private channel has a lock icon indicator in the sidebar
steps: Step 1: Create a private channel
Step 2: View the channel in the sidebar
Step 3: Verify a lock icon (🔒 or SVG lock) appears before or after the channel name
Step 4: Verify public channels do not show the lock icon

description: User profile page avatar upload area has a hover state with camera icon
steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings
Step 2: Hover over the avatar image/initials circle
Step 3: Verify a camera or edit icon overlays the avatar on hover
Step 4: Verify an Upload photo or Change avatar label appears
Step 5: Verify the overlay uses a semi-transparent dark background

description: Input fields have consistent dark styling with border and focus states
steps: Step 1: Inspect a text input on the login form
Step 2: Verify background is #1e1e1e (Surface2)
Step 3: Verify border is 1px solid #2a2a2a
Step 4: Click the input to focus it
Step 5: Verify focus ring appears in #ffffff (accent color)
Step 6: Verify the same styling applies to inputs on register and settings pages

description: New workspace button (+) in rail has a tooltip label on hover
steps: Step 1: Hover over the Add Workspace button in the workspace rail
Step 2: Verify a tooltip appears with text like Add a workspace
Step 3: Verify the tooltip uses dark background with light text
Step 4: Verify tooltip disappears when cursor moves away

description: Workspace icons in the rail have tooltips showing workspace name
steps: Step 1: Hover over a workspace icon in the rail
Step 2: Verify a tooltip appears showing the workspace name
Step 3: Verify tooltip styling matches the dark theme
Step 4: Verify the tooltip closes on mouseout

description: Skeleton loader rows match the approximate shape of real message rows
steps: Step 1: On slow network, navigate to a channel
Step 2: Observe skeleton loaders while messages fetch
Step 3: Verify skeleton rows have a circular placeholder for the avatar
Step 4: Verify skeleton rows have rectangular placeholders for username and message text
Step 5: Verify skeleton color is slightly lighter than the dark background (shimmer effect)

description: Channel browser search input is styled consistently with dark theme inputs
steps: Step 1: Open the channel browser
Step 2: Locate the search input for filtering channels
Step 3: Verify background is #1e1e1e with #2a2a2a border
Step 4: Verify placeholder text in #6b6b6b
Step 5: Verify a search icon is visible inside or adjacent to the input

description: Responsive: at 768px the 3-column layout collapses to mobile view
steps: Step 1: Set viewport to 768px wide
Step 2: Verify workspace rail and channel sidebar are hidden
Step 3: Verify only the chat main area is visible
Step 4: Verify a navigation toggle (hamburger) is visible in the header

description: Mobile sidebar drawer overlays the chat area without pushing it
steps: Step 1: Set viewport to 375px
Step 2: Tap the hamburger to open the sidebar drawer
Step 3: Verify the drawer slides over the chat (overlay mode, not push mode)
Step 4: Verify a semi-transparent backdrop appears behind the drawer
Step 5: Tap the backdrop to close the drawer

description: Status indicator text below DM recipient name is styled in muted color
steps: Step 1: User B has status set to Active now
Step 2: Open DM with User B
Step 3: Verify the header shows Active now or similar in #6b6b6b below the name
Step 4: Verify the text is smaller than the name (12px or 13px)

description: Compose box expands vertically with multi-line content up to a max height
steps: Step 1: Click the composer and type a single line
Step 2: Verify the composer is approximately 44px tall
Step 3: Press Shift+Enter to add more lines until the composer exceeds 5 lines
Step 4: Verify the composer expands up to a max height (e.g., 200px) then becomes scrollable
Step 5: Verify the message list above does not jump when the composer expands

description: Unread count badge on workspace icon is capped at 9+ visually
steps: Step 1: Generate 15 unread messages in a workspace
Step 2: View the workspace icon badge in the rail
Step 3: Verify the badge shows 9+ rather than the exact number 15
Step 4: Verify the badge is readable and does not overflow the icon

description: Profile settings page avatar is displayed at 80px or larger
steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings
Step 2: Inspect the avatar element on the settings page
Step 3: Verify the avatar is at least 80x80px
Step 4: Verify the avatar is circular with border-radius: 50%

description: Channel join button reflects TanStack Query mutation isPending state while join request is in flight
steps: Step 1: Open channel browser on slow network
Step 2: Click Join on a public channel
Step 3: Verify the Join button enters loading state when TanStack Query's useMutation isPending is true
Step 4: Verify the button's disabled attribute is bound to useMutation's isPending property, not a local useState flag
Step 5: Verify TanStack Query's onSuccess callback invalidates the channel list query, causing the browser list to refetch and remove or relabel the joined channel row

description: Message list does not show a horizontal scrollbar
steps: Step 1: Send a very long message (200+ characters on one line)
Step 2: View the message in the channel
Step 3: Verify the message wraps to the next line and does not cause horizontal scroll
Step 4: Verify overflow-x is hidden on the message list container

description: App title in the browser tab reads Colloquium Chat
steps: Step 1: Open the app
Step 2: Verify the browser tab title is Colloquium Chat
Step 3: Navigate to a channel
Step 4: Verify title becomes #channel-name - Colloquium Chat or similar

description: Settings sidebar navigation items have active state highlighting
steps: Step 1: Navigate to profile settings
Step 2: Verify the Profile nav item is highlighted as active
Step 3: Click Password nav item
Step 4: Verify Password is now highlighted and Profile is not
Step 5: Verify active item uses accent left-border or background highlight

description: Confirmation dialogs for destructive actions use modal with correct dark styling
steps: Step 1: Click Delete Channel or Leave Workspace
Step 2: Verify a confirmation modal/dialog appears
Step 3: Verify modal background is #161616 or #1e1e1e
Step 4: Verify there is a semi-transparent dark backdrop behind the modal
Step 5: Verify Confirm button is styled in danger red (#ef4444)

description: Workspace settings page has a correctly styled header with workspace name
steps: Step 1: Navigate to workspace settings
Step 2: Verify the page header shows the workspace name prominently
Step 3: Verify font-weight is bold and font-size is at least 20px
Step 4: Verify the header background matches the dark theme

description: TanStack Table member list rows align avatar, name, and role columns consistently
steps: Step 1: Open the workspace members list
Step 2: Verify TanStack Table column definitions assign correct alignment: avatar column (left), name column (center), role column (right)
Step 3: Verify vertical alignment is centered for each row element
Step 4: Verify TanStack Table does not produce variable row heights due to missing explicit row sizing in cell renderers

description: DM open button (+) in sidebar shows a tooltip on hover
steps: Step 1: Hover over the + button next to DIRECT MESSAGES in the sidebar
Step 2: Verify a tooltip appears with text like Open a direct message
Step 3: Verify tooltip styling matches the dark theme

description: Thread reply composer is visually distinct from the main channel composer
steps: Step 1: Open a thread panel
Step 2: View the thread reply composer at the bottom of the panel
Step 3: Verify it has a narrower width fitting the thread panel
Step 4: Verify the placeholder says something like Reply in thread
Step 5: Verify styling is otherwise consistent with the main composer dark theme

description: Unread indicator dot is not shown on the currently active channel
steps: Step 1: Make a channel have unread messages
Step 2: Click that channel to open it
Step 3: Verify the unread dot immediately disappears from the channel in the sidebar
Step 4: Verify the channel name returns to normal (non-bold) weight

description: Page loads without flash of white background (FOUC prevention)
steps: Step 1: Clear browser cache
Step 2: Navigate to the app on /login
Step 3: Verify no white flash occurs before the dark theme is applied
Step 4: Verify the HTML root element has a dark background-color set in the CSS or index.html

description: Prism.js syntax highlight theme matches the dark color palette
steps: Step 1: Send a code block with JavaScript code
Step 2: Inspect the code block element and its color theme
Step 3: Verify the code block background is dark (#0d0d0d or similar)
Step 4: Verify keyword tokens are highlighted but not in garish neon colors
Step 5: Verify the overall code block aesthetic is consistent with Linear/Notion minimal dark

description: Channel list in sidebar truncates long channel names with ellipsis
steps: Step 1: Create a channel with a very long name like this-is-a-very-long-channel-name
Step 2: View it in the sidebar
Step 3: Verify the channel name is truncated with ellipsis (...) if it exceeds the sidebar width
Step 4: Verify the full name is visible in a tooltip on hover

description: Messages by the same author in quick succession are visually grouped
steps: Step 1: Send 3 messages in a row from the same user within 2 minutes
Step 2: Verify the second and third messages do not repeat the avatar and username
Step 3: Verify they are visually grouped under the first message
Step 4: Verify the grouping uses reduced vertical spacing between grouped messages

description: Join workspace page (/join/:token) is styled consistently with the auth pages
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a valid invite URL /join/:token while logged out
Step 2: Verify a page appears showing workspace name and a Join or Sign in to join button
Step 3: Verify the page background is #0d0d0d
Step 4: Verify the card/content area matches the login/register card style

description: App favicon is present and distinct from default browser icon
steps: Step 1: Open the app in a browser
Step 2: Verify the browser tab shows a favicon (not the default browser icon)
Step 3: Verify the favicon is visible on both light and dark OS themes
Step 4: Verify the favicon file exists in the public directory
